content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule and can identify the neighbors of atom C:19. Looking at the image, I can see that atom C:19 (carbon with index 19) is connected to:

1. C:17 (carbon) - to the left
2. C:15 (carbon) - below and to the right
3. N:2 (nitrogen) - above and to the left

Wait, let me double-check this. Looking more carefully at the image, I see that atom C:19 is part of what appears to be a ring structure. The connections are:
- C:17 (carbon) - directly to the left
- C:15 (carbon) - directly below
- N:2 (nitrogen) - above and to the left

I should also check if there's any other connection I'm missing. Let me rotate the molecule slightly to get a better perspective on all bonds around C:19.

Actually, looking at the image again, I can clearly see that C:19 is bonded to three atoms:
- C:17 (carbon)
- C:15 (carbon) 
- N:2 (nitrogen)

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 6853, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_6853.xyz".

Let me confirm this by examining the connections once more to ensure I haven't missed any neighbors.

Based on the visual evidence, the neighbor atoms of C:19 are:
- Atom index 17, element C
- Atom index 15, element C  
- Atom index 2, element N

The geomconf_index is 6853.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 19, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
